Card of the Day Worksheets Progress Bingo

✦ Module 5 ✦

🔮

Weaving the Web

Tarot Spreads & Layouts

A tarot spread is a structured conversation with the cards. Each position in a spread creates a frame for interpretation — a lens through which you view the card's message. Learning spreads means learning to ask better questions and hear richer answers.

✦ Learning Objectives

  • ✦ Master classic tarot spreads from one-card pulls to the Celtic Cross
  • ✦ Understand how positional meanings shape card interpretation
  • ✦ Design your own custom tarot spreads for specific questions
  • ✦ Synthesize multiple cards into a cohesive, flowing reading narrative

How Spreads Work

When a card sits in a spread position, that position's meaning acts as a modifier for how you read the card. The same card in the "past" position tells a very different story than in the "outcome" position.

Learning to read spreads means developing the skill of synthesis — weaving the individual card meanings together into a coherent narrative that addresses the querent's question.

Tips for Reading Spreads

✦

First take in the spread as a whole — what's the overall feeling?

✦

Note patterns: more than 3 cards of one suit? Many reversals? Court cards?

✦

Read each card in its position, then tell the story of how they connect

✦

Let the cards in tension with each other be your most interesting clues

✦

End with a 'so what' — what is the overall message for the querent?

Spread Library

🃏

One Card Pull

1 card

Daily guidance, yes/no questions, focus of the day

Card Positions:

  1. 1. The Message — What do I need to know?
🔮

Three Card Spread

3 cards

Situation overview, past/present/future, mind/body/spirit

Card Positions:

  1. 1. Past / Situation
  2. 2. Present / Action
  3. 3. Future / Outcome
⭐

Five Card Spread

5 cards

Decision-making, deeper situation analysis

Card Positions:

  1. 1. Current situation
  2. 2. What helps you
  3. 3. What hinders you
  4. 4. Advice
  5. 5. Likely outcome
✚

Celtic Cross

10 cards

Deep comprehensive reading on any life situation

Card Positions:

  1. 1. The Present — The heart of the matter
  2. 2. The Challenge — What crosses or complicates you
  3. 3. The Past — Recent past influences
  4. 4. The Future — Near future
  5. 5. Above — Your conscious goal or ideal
  6. 6. Below — Unconscious influences or foundation
  7. 7. Advice — How to proceed
  8. 8. External Influences — Environment and others
  9. 9. Hopes & Fears — What you hope or fear
  10. 10. Outcome — The final result
🌙

Horseshoe Spread

7 cards

Balanced view of a question or situation

Card Positions:

  1. 1. Past
  2. 2. Present
  3. 3. Hidden influences
  4. 4. Obstacles
  5. 5. Attitudes of others
  6. 6. What to do
  7. 7. Outcome
💞

Relationship Spread

6 cards

Understanding dynamics between two people

Card Positions:

  1. 1. How you see yourself
  2. 2. How you see the other
  3. 3. How they see you
  4. 4. How they see themselves
  5. 5. The connection between you
  6. 6. What the relationship needs

🎯 Activity: Design Your Own Spread

Think of a question or area of life you want the tarot to illuminate. Then design a spread specifically for it.

Your spread should include:

  1. A title and clear purpose for the spread
  2. 3-7 card positions with specific meanings
  3. A layout (can be any shape — cross, circle, line, star)
  4. At least one "hidden influences" or "what you don't know" position

Use the "My Personal Spread Creation" worksheet from the Worksheets page.

💬 Discussion Prompts

  • Q:What spread format feels most natural to your reading style?
  • Q:How does the context of positions change card meanings for you?
  • Q:What kind of questions do you most want to ask the cards?
